title: Cost of Capital: If a firm has a current dividend of $5, a...
summary: Calculate or evaluate the cost of capital using appropriate financial models.
prompt: If a firm has a current dividend of $5, a required return of 12%, and a constant growth rate of 4%, what is the stock price using the Gordon Growth Model?
solution: Price = D1 / (k - g) = $5 × (1 + 0.04) / (0.12 - 0.04) = $5.20 / 0.08 = $65. This value provides insight into the stock's worth based on future dividend payments and their growth.
